address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-07 14:43:46 -0700.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Unprocessed collection. Material is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Irina Kosmovska, born in Moscow, circa 1914, immigrated to Berlin during the Russian Revolution, where she studied with Mariinsky ballerinas Ola Preobrajenska and Lubov Egorova. Debuted in 1936 with Col. de Basil's Ballet Russe de Monte Carlo, then partnered with Paul Goube in Barcelona, where she also began to teach and choreograph ballet works. Kosmovska was sponsored by impresario Sol Hurok to come to the U.S. in 1951, settling in California, where she taught for Marge and Gower Champion, and studied with Carmelita Maracci. In 1960, she founded the Los Angeles Junior Ballet, which existed through 1985, and became the official school of John Clifford's Los Angeles Ballet. In the early 1960s, Kosmovska taught for George Balanchine's School of the American Ballet in New York, and with American Ballet Theatre (ABT) students in Los Angeles and in Saratoga in the summers. She was recognized as "a revered expert on instruction in the pirouette."

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection contains photographs, correspondence, manuscripts, programs, scrapbooks, and programs documenting the life and work of Irina Kosmovska, Ballet Russes de Monte Carlo soloist, teacher (Balanchine's School of American Ballet in New York, and Los Angeles Junior Ballet in Los Angeles), choreographer, and founder of the Los Angeles Junior Ballet.

controlaccess.subject: Ballet -- Study and teaching -- California -- Los Angeles.
controlaccess.occupation: Ballet dancers -- California -- Los Angeles -- Archives.
